item.title: Harvard will cap number of A's awarded to students. Here's why.
item.description: Harvard University faculty members voted to cap the number of A's awarded to students in an effort to stop grade inflation.

item.title: Ukraine ups security as Russia holds nuclear strike drills with Belarus
item.description: Russia says "nuclear munitions" sent to Belarus for joint drills in the country that Moscow used as a launchpad for its 2022 invasion of Ukraine.

item.title: Iran mulls latest U.S. proposal as Trump says war is in "final stages"
item.description: Iran says it's considering the latest U.S. peace offer, as President Trump says he's willing to wait "a couple of days" for a response.

item.title: Extended interview: Cheyenne Hunt on Reckoning Action organization
item.description: Cheyenne Hunt, the founder of Reckoning Action, an organization that confronts misogyny in America by pushing for legislative changes and supporting abuse survivors, speaks to CBS News' Nancy Cordes about her mission to combat gender bias.

item.title: SpaceX files for biggest-ever IPO ahead of market debut
item.description: Elon Musk's SpaceX is moving ahead with plans to go public in what some expect will be the biggest IPO ever.

item.title: Record 274 climbers scale Nepal's side of Mount Everest in single day
item.description: A record 274 climbers scaled the Nepal side of Mount Everest in a single day, officials said. They took advantage of clear weather.

item.title: Sneak peek: Kouri Richins: Behind the Facade
item.description: ALL NEW: Scared of their own mother. Why the children of convicted killer Kouri Richins want her to stay behind bars. "48 Hours" correspondent Natalie Morales reports Saturday, May 23 at 10/9c* on CBS and streaming on Paramount+. [Airs following the WNBA on CBS)

item.title: Daycare owner charged with fraud tried to flee U.S. after closing center, prosecutors say
item.description: New fraud charges were unsealed Wednesday against a Minnesota daycare owner who federal prosecutors allege tried to flee the country just two days after shutting the center down.

item.title: "Peanuts" music owner sues U.S. government over use of iconic songs
item.description: Lee Mendelson Film Productions alleges the U.S. Department of the Interior illegally used the jazzy tunes in social media posts and a video game.

item.title: Colbert hosts "The Late Show" one last time tonight as franchise ends
item.description: "The Late Show" host Stephen Colbert is marking the end of an iconic late-night franchise on CBS.

item.title: Is Trump's $1.7+ billion "anti-weaponization fund" legal? Experts weigh in.
item.description: The new fund to provide payouts to those who say the legal system was "weaponized" against them raised immediate questions about its legality, implementation and enforcement.

item.title: World Cup expected to generate more in sports bets than the Super Bowl
item.description: Americans are expected to wager more than $3 billion amid the expansion of legalized sports betting in the U.S.

item.title: These are retirees' 5 biggest financial fears
item.description: Retirees say inflation, health care costs and market volatility are threatening their financial security.

item.title: Trump says Netanyahu will do "whatever I want him to do" on Iran
item.description: Asked what he said to Israel's leader about a decision to hold off on new Iran strikes, Trump said Netanyahu will "do whatever I want him to do."

item.title: Detroit-bound flight diverted over passenger from Congo amid Ebola restrictions
item.description: An Air France flight from Paris to Detroit was forced to divert to Montreal due to U.S. flight restrictions linked to the Ebola outbreak.

item.title: U.S. indicts Cuba's Raúl Castro for murder over downing of planes in 1996
item.description: Former Cuban leader Raúl Castro was indicted by a U.S. grand jury in connection with the Cuban military's fatal downing of two planes in 1996 — an escalation in the U.S. pressure campaign against the Cuban government.

item.title: State Democratic Party censures governor after he commutes Tina Peters' sentence
item.description: The central committee of the Colorado Democratic Party on Wednesday voted 89.8% in favor of a measure to censure Gov. Jared Polis.

item.title: New version of Starship rocket ready for critical test flight
item.description: The new rocket features a host of upgrades intended to improve safety and performance of the world's most powerful rocket.

item.title: DOJ veterans fear probe into ex-CIA director is being stacked with Trump loyalists
item.description: Former CIA Director John Brennan is the subject of two criminal probes being led by the Miami-area U.S. Attorney's Office.

item.title: DHS to tighten Ebola-related flight restrictions for some foreign travelers
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security is set to implement new entry restrictions beginning Thursday for foreign travelers coming to the U.S. from countries at the center of the latest Ebola outbreak.

item.title: Byron Allen on "Comics Unleashed" as it moves to new time slot on CBS
item.description: Comedian and media mogul Byron Allen brings "Comics Unleashed" to CBS's late night slot, replacing "The Late Show With Stephen Colbert."

item.title: Highest gas prices in years expected to continue rising this summer
item.description: U.S. motorists are likely to face even hotter gas prices as the summer driving season kicks off, according to a new analysis.

item.title: The story of Cuba's 1996 shootdown that led to Raúl Castro's indictment
item.description: Thirty years ago, a Cuban fighter jet shot down two civilian planes operated by Florida-based exile group Brothers to the Rescue, an incident that inflamed U.S.-Cuba relations.

item.title: Noah Wyle on his push to help healthcare workers
item.description: Noah Wyle, who stars in HBO's "The Pitt," joins "CBS Mornings" with Dr. Elisabeth Potter to discuss their push to improve mental health and quality of life for healthcare workers.

item.title: Ebola outbreak declared a global health emergency by WHO
item.description: Outbreaks of the deadly Ebola virus in two African countries have prompted the World Health Organization to declare a global health emergency. Imtiaz Tyab reports.

item.title: 5/16: CBS Weekend News
item.description: Sailors on USS Gerald R. Ford come home after their historic mission; new uncertainty for America's farmers.

item.title: Extended interview: Noah Wyle
item.description: In this web exclusive, CBS News chief medical correspondent Dr. Jon LaPook talks with Noah Wyle, the star of "ER" and the HBO Max series "The Pitt," about how his new show depicts the fragility of the medical system, and about his character, Dr. Michael "Robby" Robinavitch.

item.title: Behind the scenes of "The Pitt"
item.description: The Emmy-winning HBO Max drama "The Pitt" immerses viewers in the hour-by-hour struggles faced by the overworked-yet-superhuman emergency room staff at the fictional Pittsburgh Trauma Medical Center. CBS News chief medical correspondent Dr. Jon LaPook visits the series' hyper-realistic set at Warner Bros. Studios in Burbank, Calif. (where cast members undergo a two-week medical boot camp), and talks with star, writer, director and executive producer Noah Wyle about why the former "ER" actor returned to the medical drama genre.

item.title: Noah Wyle talks authenticity of medical drama "The Pitt" and how cast prepared
item.description: The hit series "The Pitt" has earned praise for its realistic look at the pressures facing health care workers. Chief medical correspondent Dr. Jon LaPook spoke to the star of the show, Noah Wyle, for "CBS Sunday Morning." Wyle talked about how the cast prepared for their roles beyond learning their lines before shooting the series even began.

item.title: Noah Wyle, who stars in medical drama, wants more support for health care workers like his mom
item.description: Actor Noah Wyle stars in the medical drama hit show "The Pitt." On Thursday, Wyle and his mother, who has been a nurse for four decades, will be in Washington D.C. speaking with lawmakers about policy changes that could help health care workers. The two speak to "CBS Mornings" about support for medical workers.
